title: Trinity Sunday
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## June 12, 2022 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

Trinity Sunday fell on June 12, 2022, the first Sunday after Pentecost. The Revised Common Lectionary readings were Isaiah 40:12-17, 27-31; Psalm 8; 2 Corinthians 13:11-13; and Matthew 28:16-20. The church was in Ordinary Time, having completed the Easter season the previous week, making this the traditional Sunday for deep theological reflection on the doctrine of the Trinity.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the lead-up to its triennial General Convention, scheduled for July 2022 in Baltimore, where significant debates on LGBTQ+ inclusion and prayer book revision were anticipated. The Diocese of Colorado had been navigating leadership transitions and post-pandemic recovery efforts throughout the spring. Broderick Greer, serving as Canon for Racial Reconciliation and Justice in the Diocese, was increasingly visible in diocesan conversations around racial justice initiatives.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver experienced unseasonably warm weather in early June 2022, with temperatures in the high 80s. The city was preparing for Pride Month celebrations, with the Denver Pride Festival scheduled for mid-June in Civic Center Park, just blocks from the Cathedral. Housing affordability and homelessness remained pressing local issues, with Capitol Hill experiencing visible increases in unhoused populations in the downtown corridor.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Supreme Court had just overturned Roe v. Wade on June 24 (12 days after this sermon), but in the week prior, the decision's imminent announcement created national tension and protests. The January 6 Committee held its first prime-time televised hearing on June 9, 2022, presenting evidence about the Capitol riot. Inflation remained at a 40-year high, dominating economic news and political discourse heading into the 2022 midterm election cycle.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Russia's invasion of Ukraine continued its fourth month with intense fighting in the Donbas region and ongoing humanitarian crises affecting millions of civilians. The war had already triggered global energy and food security crises affecting prices worldwide. The UK was in the midst of political turmoil, with Boris Johnson's government facing multiple scandals and the first waves of ministerial resignations (which accelerated significantly in the following week).

CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture in June 2022 was marked by deep political polarization, pandemic fatigue mixed with lingering anxiety, and growing concerns about recession. The zeitgeist reflected tension between Pride Month celebrations and impending abortion restrictions, creating sharp cultural fault lines in religious and secular communities. Sports attention was divided between NBA Finals and the World Cup preparation, while the Supreme Court's anticipated Roe decision hung over the national conversation like an approaching storm.
